target: gramps-6.1 worktree — upstream/maintenance/gramps61 (unmodified), realigned by `make preflight`
note: Known pre-existing reds on the unmodified target tree (testbed issue #7, act-log 2026-06-06). A run that matches this baseline is environmental, not a regression — only a NEW failure raises §6. known_failures are auto-captured by `make preflight` (t3_baseline --update) on the realigned gramps-6.1 worktree; baseline_tree pins the SHA they were recorded against (a tree drift warns — #89). The 7 captured reds are two clusters: (1) the 5 legacy-bsddb import tests `test_imp_*_zip` fail because the bsddb backend (berkeleydb/bsddb3) is not installable in the image, so db_load falls through to make_database('bsddb') and the test fails instead of skipping — fixed upstream-pending by bug #13260 / commit b932443f15 (when that lands upstream, the next preflight realign clears these and t3_baseline prints 'recorded red cleared (shrink the manifest?)'); (2) `reports_test.TestDynamic::test_WebCal` / `::test_navwebpage` are environmental dynamic-report reds on the unmodified tree. run_level_signatures stays as the fallback for the discovery segfault (seen on some runs instead of per-test failures). Re-promote this gate to gating once the suite is green-baseline (pdca.toml).

run_level_signatures.cause: core unit suite segfaults during unittest/xmlrunner discovery; underlying defect not yet isolated
run_level_signatures.tracking: act-log 2026-06-06 open Act item (isolate on an unmodified maintenance/gramps61 checkout, then file as a core bug)
